evals.prompt: I'm writing a new compiled PowerShell cmdlet in C# called Remove-StaleCache. It needs to support -WhatIf and -Confirm, accept a pipeline of string paths, and return a result object per path. Should I inherit from Cmdlet or PSCmdlet? Show me the class skeleton with the correct [Cmdlet] attribute, [OutputType], base class, and ProcessRecord override.
expected_sections: Base class recommendation with rationale
Cmdlet attribute declaration (verb, noun, SupportsShouldProcess)
OutputType declaration
ProcessRecord override skeleton
Pipeline binding on the Path parameter
ShouldProcess call pattern inside ProcessRecord
assertions: Recommends PSCmdlet (not Cmdlet) — the SDK documentation requires PSCmdlet as the base class for cmdlets that call ShouldProcess, because PSCmdlet provides the full runtime context ShouldProcess needs at execution time
Declares [Cmdlet(VerbsCommon.Remove, "StaleCache", SupportsShouldProcess = true)] on the class
Includes [OutputType(typeof(...))] attribute above the class
ProcessRecord override calls $PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess or this.ShouldProcess before performing the destructive operation
Path parameter uses [Parameter(ValueFromPipeline = true)] or [Parameter(ValueFromPipelineByPropertyName = true)]
Does not use Write-Host or Console.WriteLine for output — uses WriteObject

evals.prompt: Write a PowerShell advanced function called Invoke-ResourceProvisioning that takes a mandatory -ResourceName string and an optional -Force switch. It should support -WhatIf/-Confirm for the provisioning step, throw a terminating error if the resource name is null or empty after trimming, and emit a non-terminating error if provisioning fails but -Force is not set. Use [CmdletBinding()] and the full cmdlet contract.
expected_sections: CmdletBinding declaration with SupportsShouldProcess
Parameter block with Mandatory and Force switch
Begin or Process block structure
ShouldProcess call before provisioning
Terminating error via $PSCmdlet.ThrowTerminatingError with ErrorRecord
Non-terminating error via $PSCmdlet.WriteError with ErrorRecord
ErrorRecord construction (exception, errorId, ErrorCategory, targetObject)
assertions: Declares [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess = $true)] — not just [CmdletBinding()]
Uses $PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess($ResourceName, 'Provision resource') before the state-changing call
Constructs an [System.Management.Automation.ErrorRecord] with a typed exception, string errorId, ErrorCategory enum value, and target object
Uses $PSCmdlet.ThrowTerminatingError(...) for the null/empty guard — not throw or Write-Error
Uses $PSCmdlet.WriteError(...) for the non-fatal provisioning failure path
Does not use Write-Host for status output in the pipeline path

evals.prompt: Write a [CmdletBinding()] advanced function called Get-StaleFileReport that accepts a mandatory -SourcePath string, a mandatory -OlderThanDays positive integer, and an optional -IncludeHidden flag. The function should enumerate all files under -SourcePath recursively, filter to those whose LastWriteTime is older than -OlderThanDays days, and emit one [PSCustomObject] per matching file with Name, FullPath, AgeDays, and SizeBytes properties. Include a complete comment-based help block with all required sections. Declare the output type explicitly. Use the correct type for the optional flag parameter.
expected_sections: Comment-based help block placed immediately inside the function body before the param block
All six required help sections: .SYNOPSIS, .DESCRIPTION, one .PARAMETER entry per public parameter, at least one .EXAMPLE, .OUTPUTS, and .NOTES
[OutputType] attribute declaration consistent with the .OUTPUTS type
CmdletBinding declaration
Parameter block with Mandatory attributes and [switch] for -IncludeHidden
Process block using Get-ChildItem and Where-Object with full cmdlet names
PSCustomObject construction emitted into the pipeline
assertions: Comment-based help block (<# ... #>) appears inside the function body before the param block — not outside or adjacent to the function

Help block contains all six required sections: .SYNOPSIS, .DESCRIPTION, a .PARAMETER entry for every public parameter (-SourcePath, -OlderThanDays, -IncludeHidden), at least one .EXAMPLE with expected output, .OUTPUTS naming the concrete emitted type, and .NOTES
.OUTPUTS names the same concrete type as the [OutputType] attribute — the two declarations are consistent with each other
[OutputType] is not [object] or [PSObject]; a concrete or descriptive type is declared
-IncludeHidden is declared as [switch], not [bool] or [System.Boolean]
Script body uses Get-ChildItem (not gci, ls, or dir) with explicit -Path and -Recurse named parameters
Script body uses Where-Object (not ? or where) for filtering
Script body does not call Read-Host anywhere
Script body does not use the ForEach-Object alias % or a bare foreach pipeline alias in place of ForEach-Object

expected_sections: Enumeration of every documentation and style violation found
Missing .OUTPUTS section in the help block
Missing .NOTES section in the help block
Missing .PARAMETER entry for -CaseSensitive
Missing [OutputType] attribute
Read-Host call that blocks non-interactive execution
[bool] parameter that should be [switch]
Alias usages: gci, %, ?
Corrected function with all violations resolved
assertions: Identifies the missing .OUTPUTS section — the help block does not document what type the function emits
Identifies the missing .NOTES section — the help block omits the required author/version notes entry
Identifies the missing .PARAMETER entry for -CaseSensitive — a public parameter with no corresponding help documentation
Identifies the missing [OutputType] attribute and adds [OutputType([string])] (or a more specific type) aligned with the .OUTPUTS text in the corrected code
Identifies the Read-Host call as a violation — it blocks non-interactive execution; all required input must arrive via declared parameters
Identifies [bool]$CaseSensitive as a violation and replaces it with [switch]$CaseSensitive in the corrected code
Identifies gci as an alias for Get-ChildItem and replaces it with Get-ChildItem using explicit named parameters in the corrected code
Identifies % as an alias for ForEach-Object and replaces it with ForEach-Object in the corrected code
Identifies ? as an alias for Where-Object and replaces it with Where-Object in the corrected code
Corrected code does not contain Read-Host
Corrected code declares [switch]$CaseSensitive (no default value needed; [switch] implies $false when absent)
Corrected code includes both .OUTPUTS and .NOTES in the help block

expected_sections: Scope assessment — whether this falls within the skill boundary
Identification that the function lacks [CmdletBinding()] and does not follow the cmdlet contract
Redirect or note pointing to the appropriate guidance resource
assertions: Identifies that the function is outside this skill's scope because it does not declare [CmdletBinding()]
Does NOT apply cmdlet-contract rules (ErrorRecord, ThrowTerminatingError, ShouldProcess) as if this were a compiled cmdlet or advanced function
States clearly that generic PowerShell scripts without [CmdletBinding()] are explicitly out of scope per the skill description

If offering any improvement, suggests adding [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess = $true)] and WhatIf support as the path to bring the function into scope — or defers to a general PowerShell resource
